summary: Wide fighter-character cleanup touching 68 files under src/melee/ft/chara plus ftcliffcommon. The main mechanical change replaced comparisons such as ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) == 0 or == FALSE with native boolean negation, because review clarified ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining returns a BOOL rather than a frame count. The PR also removed unnecessary casts/parentheses and simplified float literals, including changing an exact radian literal to an expression like 60 * M_PI / 180 in Marth SpecialN wind-effect code.
what_changed: Replaced many end-of-animation checks of the form ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) == 0 or == FALSE with !ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj).
Applied this boolean cleanup broadly across Captain, CLink, Dr Mario, Fox, Game & Watch, Koopa, Luigi, Mario, Marth/Mars, Master Hand, Mewtwo, Ness, Samus, Yoshi, Zelda, and ftcliffcommon files.
Simplified float literals per PR body; a visible example changed 1.0471975803375244f to 60 * M_PI / 180 in src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/ftMarsSpecialN.c.
Removed unnecessary casts and unnecessary parentheses according to the PR body, though the provided diff excerpt mainly shows the ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining boolean conversions.
Kept changes source-level and readability-oriented, with roughly balanced additions/deletions: 298 added and 303 deleted across 68 changed files.
smart_moves: Used the semantic type of ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining as the basis for cleanup: treating the result as BOOL made !ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) clearer than comparing to FALSE or 0.
Applied reviewer feedback project-wide rather than only at the commented hunk; the author noted there were many occurrences and that they were handled broadly.
Converted an opaque high-precision radian constant into an angle expression using M_PI, making intent easier to recognize without changing the represented value in source intent.
Made a large, repetitive cleanup mechanically consistent across many character modules, which improves future grep/search patterns for animation callback completion checks.
decomp_lessons: When a helper returns BOOL, prefer direct boolean operators such as !helper(...) instead of equality comparisons against FALSE or 0; this communicates the type correctly and matches reviewer guidance from this PR.
Do not infer that a function name containing FramesRemaining means the return should be treated as a numeric frame count; review explicitly called out ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining as BOOL.
For decomp readability, replace unexplained decimal approximations of common angles with symbolic expressions when the expression preserves intent, as with 60 * M_PI / 180.
Large cleanup PRs are safest when they are narrow and mechanical: this one consistently targeted casts, parentheses, bool comparisons, and literals rather than changing behavior.

Future ft/chara work should grep for repeated idioms across all character folders after review comments identify a pattern, because a single callback idiom often appears in many fighters' animation callbacks.
naming_conventions: Use ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) as a boolean predicate in conditionals; the cleaned-up convention is if (!ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj)) for animation-complete checks.

The diff keeps existing fighter module naming styles such as ftFox_SpecialN.c, ftGameWatch_SpecialLw.c, ftLuigi_SpecialS.c, ftMarsSpecialN.c, and ftMasterHand_30.c; the PR does not provide evidence of file or symbol renames.
The review reinforces that BOOL-returning APIs should be named/used as predicates even if the phrase 'FramesRemaining' sounds count-like.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was documented beyond source cleanup.

The cleanup suggests a matching-safe tactic for decomp code: simplify boolean comparisons and redundant syntax when the generated code remains acceptable, but the provided context does not include build/match proof details.

Replacing a decimal float approximation with an expression involving M_PI can improve readability for known angles; future work should verify generated constants before applying this tactic in matching-sensitive code.
review_feedback: ribbanya reviewed src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/ftMarsSpecialN.c and stated that ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining returns BOOL, not a frame count, so replacing ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) == 0 with !ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) is safe.
The reviewer noted the BOOL comparison pattern appeared quite a lot, implying the cleanup should be applied broadly.
r-burns responded that there were many such occurrences and that they believed they took care of all of them.
follow_up_queries: grep for "ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(.*) == FALSE" or "ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(.*) == 0" in later branches to find missed or reintroduced style regressions
inspect whether other BOOL-returning fighter helpers are still compared to FALSE or 0
search for high-precision radian literals in ft/chara that could be clearer as degree-to-radian expressions, while verifying matching
review later ft/chara cleanup PRs for continued convention around predicate-style animation completion checks
